{{Quote|Remember Jorman Thoad when you have ''other'' luxury yachts to trade for modest but dependable crafts!|Jorman Thoad|<ref name="marvel25" />}}
'''Jorman Thoad''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who ran a [[Starship/Legends|starship]] dealership on the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Centares/Legends|Centares]]. By [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]], Thoad claimed to be Centares' largest dealer in used spacecraft, and often referred customers to a [[Jorman Thoad's cousin|cousin]] who dealt [[Droid/Legends|droids]] in Centares' [[Old Town (Centares)|Old Town]]. That [[Standard year/Legends|year]], Thoad made a rather lucrative trade when he met two [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] leaders, [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]], who were looking to offload a flashy [[Spoilt Sport|yacht]] while evading the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire]]. Thoad took the [[Yacht/Legends|yacht]] in exchange for a less-expensive [[Luke Skywalker's scout craft|scout craft]], which carried with it a much lower profile. A passing remark Thoad made during the exchange turned the Rebels' attention to an Imperial [[Yavin blockade|blockade]] of the Rebel-held [[Yavin system/Legends|Yavin system]], sending them scurrying back to their base on [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]].<ref name="marvel25" />

==Behind the scenes==
Jorman Thoad made his first appearance in [[Star Wars (1977) 25|''Star Wars'' (1977) 25]], written by [[Archie Goodwin]] and released in [[1979]]. Thoad was illustrated by [[Carmine Infantino]].<ref name="marvel25" />
